title: A Chimeric Rather Than Native Secretion Signal Elicits Stress-Related Transcriptional Adaption during 75k γ-Secalin Production in 𝘒𝘰𝘮𝘢𝘨𝘢𝘵𝘢𝘦𝘭𝘭𝘢 𝘱𝘩𝘢𝘧𝘧𝘪𝘪
author: Büchner, Kai Rupert
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
The prolamin 75k γ-secalin of rye ( Secale cereale ) cultivar Elias was produced in  Komagataella phaffii  Mut S  strain BSYBG11. In total, three different mutants were investigated: i) the producer of the native 75k γ-secalin  K. phaffii  gSec, ii) the strain  K. phaffii  S4gSec, where the native γ-secalin secretion signal was replaced by the chimeric OST1-pre-MATα-pro leader, and iii) the null-mutant  K. phaffii  ΔgSec, where the target gene is replaced by a truncated version without ATG start codons.  
 Cultures were grown in baffled shaker flasks (500 mL volume filled with 1/10 of the total capacity) in buffered minimal medium at pH 6, 28°C and 180 rpm. Four samples per strain were harvested 96 h post-induction (0.5% vol. MeOH every 12 h), total RNA was extracted with the  Roboklon Universal RNA Kit according to   the   manufacturer's instructions (Roboklon GmbH, Germany) , and sequencing libraries were constructed using the  Illumina stranded mRNA Prep   kit (Illumina, USA). mRNA libraries were    sequenced on an Illumina platform NextSeq 1000 with P2 XLEAP-SBS Reagents (Illumina, USA) as dark cycle sequencing, generating paired-end reads of 99 bp length.
steps_raw:
The prolamin 75k γ-secalin of rye ( Secale cereale ) cultivar Elias was produced in  Komagataella phaffii  Mut S  strain BSYBG11. In total, three different mutants were investigated: i) the producer of the native 75k γ-secalin  K. phaffii  gSec, ii) the strain  K. phaffii  S4gSec, where the native γ-secalin secretion signal was replaced by the chimeric OST1-pre-MATα-pro leader, and iii) the null-mutant  K. phaffii  ΔgSec, where the target gene is replaced by a truncated version without ATG start codons.  
 Cultures were grown in baffled shaker flasks (500 mL volume filled with 1/10 of the total capacity) in buffered minimal medium at pH 6, 28°C and 180 rpm. Four samples per strain were harvested 96 h post-induction (0.5% vol. MeOH every 12 h), total RNA was extracted with the  Roboklon Universal RNA Kit according to   the   manufacturer's instructions (Roboklon GmbH, Germany) , and sequencing libraries were constructed using the  Illumina stranded mRNA Prep   kit (Illumina, USA). mRNA libraries were    sequenced on an Illumina platform NextSeq 1000 with P2 XLEAP-SBS Reagents (Illumina, USA) as dark cycle sequencing, generating paired-end reads of 99 bp length.
